name: 压强
subject: physics
curriculum_points: 通过实验，理解压强。知道增大和减小压强的方法，并了解其在生产生活中的应用。
估测自己站立时对地面的压强。

excerpts.text: 通过实验，理解压强。知道增大和减小压强的方法，并了解其在生产生活中的应用。
估测自己站立时对地面的压强。

interactive_resources.description: PhET 液体压强与流速（含帕斯卡定律）

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 压强

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: 初中
- **年级**: 八年级
- **章节**: 力学
- **知识点**: 压强
- **课标版本**: 义务教育课程标准（2022年版）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
>
2.2.7 通过实验，理解压强。知道增大和减小压强的方法，并了解其在生产生活中的应用。

例8 估测自己站立时对地面的压强。
>
---
>
### 1.2 学业要求
>
同机械运动学业要求
>
---
>
### 1.3 教学提示
>
同机械运动教学提示
---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解压强的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解压强的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法
- 学生能够运用物理学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验物理探究的乐趣，树立学好物理的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 压强的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构
- 压强的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

### 3.2 教学难点
- 掌握压强的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用
- 掌握压强的基本运算和简单应用
- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

## 五、教学建议（学科化补灌）

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「压强」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“通过实验，理解压强。知道增大和减小压强的方法，并了解其在生产生活中的应用。”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

deep_textbook_snippets.text:
![Figure 175 - A figure or diagram (300×376px) related to: minutes, arrive,](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0175_c8551e7f.png)
Watt’s Steam Engine James Watt did not invent the steam engine, but by the time he was finished tinkering with it, it was more useful. The first steam engines were not only inefficient, they only produced a back and forth, or reciprocal, motion. This was natural because pistons move in and out as the pressure in the chamber changes. This limitation was okay for simple tasks like pumping water or mashing potatoes, but did not work so well for moving a train. Watt was able build a steam engine that converted reciprocal motion to circular motion. With that one innovation, the industrial revolution was off and running. The world would never be the same. One of Watt's steam engines is shown in Figure 9.5. The video that follows the figure explains the importance of the steam engine in the industrial revolution. Figure 9.5 A late version of the Watt steam engine. (Nehemiah Hawkins, Wikimedia Commons)
Watt's Role in the Industrial Revolution This video demonstrates how the watts that resulted from Watt's inventions helped make the industrial revolution possible and allowed England to enter a new historical era. Click to view content (https://www.youtube.com/embed/zhL5DCizj5c)
Which form of mechanical energy does the steam engine generate? a. Potential energy b. Kinetic energy c. Nuclear energy d. Solar energy Before proceeding, be sure you understand the distinctions among force, work, energy, and power. Force exerted on an object over a distance d

The RHIC Collider Figure 10.9 shows the Brookhaven National Laboratory in Upton, NY. The circular structure houses a particle accelerator called the RHIC, which stands for Relativistic Heavy Ion Collider. The heavy ions in the name are gold nuclei that have been stripped of their electrons. Streams of ions are accelerated in several stages before entering the big ring seen in the figure. Here, they are accelerated to their final speed, which is about 99.7 percent the speed of light. Such high speeds are called relativistic. All the relativistic phenomena we have been discussing in this chapter are very pronounced in this case. At this speed = 12.9, so that relativistic time dilates by a factor of about 13, and relativistic length contracts by the same factor. Figure 10.9 Brookhaven National Laboratory. The circular structure houses the RHIC. (energy.gov, Wikimedia Commons) Two ion beams circle the 2.4-mile long track around the big ring in opposite directions. The paths can then be made to cross, thereby causing ions to collide. The collision event is very short-lived but amazingly intense. The temperatures and pressures produced are greater than those in the hottest suns. At 4 trillion degrees Celsius, this is the hottest material ever created in a 10.4 10.2 • Consequences of Special Relativity
> **[Figure 193, Page 331]** A figure or diagram (300×300px) related to: energy, simply, correct, form, Einstein, famous. This image appears on page 331 of the textbook.
![Figure 193 - A figure or diagram (300×300px) related to: energy, simply, ](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/i

The zeroth law of thermodynamics is very similar to the transitive property of equality in mathematics: If a = b and b = c, then a = c. You may be wondering at this point, why the wacky name? Shouldn’t this be called the first law of thermodynamics rather than the zeroth? The explanation is that this law was discovered after the first and second laws of thermodynamics but is so fundamental that scientists decided it should logically come first. As an example of the zeroth law in action, consider newborn babies in neonatal intensive-care units in hospitals. Prematurely born or sick newborns are placed in special incubators. These babies have very little covering while in the incubators, so to an observer, they look as though they may not be warm enough. However, inside the incubator, the temperature of the air, the cot, and the baby are all the same—that is, they are in thermal equilibrium. The ambient temperature is just high enough to keep the baby safe and comfortable.
Thermodynamics Engineer Thermodynamics engineers apply the principles of thermodynamics to mechanical systems so as to create or test products that rely on the interactions between heat, work, pressure, temperature, and volume. This type of work typically takes place in the aerospace industry, chemical manufacturing companies, industrial manufacturing plants, power plants (Figure 12.2), engine manufacturers, or electronics companies.

What would be an example of something a thermodynamics engineer would do in the aeronautics industry? a. Test the fuel efficiency of a jet engine b. Test the functioning of landing gear c. Test the functioning of a lift control device d. Test the autopilot functions Check Your Understanding 1. What is thermal equilibrium? a. When two objects in contact with each other are at the same pressure, they are said to be in thermal equilibrium. b. When two objects in contact with each other are at different temperatures, they are said to be in thermal equilibrium. c. When two objects in contact with each other are at the same temperature, they are said to be in thermal equilibrium. d. When two objects not in contact with each other are at the same pressure, they are said to be in thermal equilibrium. 2. What is the zeroth law of thermodynamics? 12.1 • Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics: Thermal Equilibrium
> **[Figure 207, Page 371]** A figure or diagram (300×449px) related to: Figure, engineer, makes, site, visit, Baghdad. This image appears on page 371 of the textbook.
![Figure 207 - A figure or diagram (300×449px) related to: Figure, engineer](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_images/img_0207_c8551e7f.png)

deep_worked_example.stem: 根据教材片段，设计一道关于「压强」的现象解释或实验探究题。
deep_worked_example.solution_outline: 先描述现象或实验条件，再指出关键概念和证据，最后给出解释或结论。

Watt’s Steam Engine James Watt did not invent the steam engine, but by the time he was finished tinkering with it, it was mor

errors.description: 只套用「压强」相关公式，忽略物理情境、单位和适用条件。
实验分析时没有控制变量，导致无法判断因果关系。

exercises.stem: 围绕「压强」，写出一个可观察现象，并说明其中涉及的物理量或规律。
exercises.answer: 应扣住课标要点：通过实验，理解压强。知道增大和减小压强的方法，并了解其在生产生活中的应用。，说明现象、条件和规律。

exercises.stem: 如果要验证「压强」相关规律，你会控制哪些变量？记录哪些数据？
exercises.answer: 需说明实验目的、控制变量、测量数据、可能误差和结论依据。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「压强」相关的物理规律。
